proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e == 0`.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines without EGFR amplification.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines without ERBB2 amplification.
Cell lines with `kras_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines without KRAS mutation.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: `braf_v600e == 1` lines had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.805 vs -0.250; effect -0.555 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: `egfr_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.601 vs -0.252; effect -0.349 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: `erbb2_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.686 vs -0.253; effect -0.432 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: `kras_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.559 vs -0.252; effect -0.307 (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `nf1_loss == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines without NF1 loss.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification == 1` and `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than other cell lines.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification == 1` have slightly more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than cell lines without MYC amplification.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: `nf1_loss == 1` lines had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.353 vs -0.257; effect -0.096 (p=2.03e-70).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: The MYC-amplified/high-8q24 subgroup had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.365 vs -0.252; effect -0.112 (p=3.53e-148).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: `myc_amplification == 1` lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.349 vs -0.331; effect -0.018 (p=1.68e-05).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Colorectal lineage cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `apc_mutation == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than APC-wild-type cell lines.
Cell lines with `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than lower-WNT cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.720 vs -0.252; effect -0.468 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: `apc_mutation == 1` lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.545 vs -0.253; effect -0.292 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: High-WNT lines had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.469 vs -0.253; effect -0.217 (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Colorectal cell lines with `apc_mutation == 1` and `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` have much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Within colorectal APC-mutant cell lines, `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` enables stronger KIF18A dependency than `wnt_activity_score < 0.819`.

High WNT alone is insufficient: cell lines with `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` but lacking the combined colorectal/APC-mutant context have less negative `dependency_KIF18A` than the full colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT subgroup.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.112 vs -0.253; effect -0.859 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast within the stated subset; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Within colorectal APC-mutant lines, high WNT had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.112 vs -0.273; effect -0.839 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast within the stated subset; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Among high-WNT lines, the full colorectal/APC-mutant subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.112 vs -0.255 in high-WNT lines outside that context; effect -0.857 (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Fast-growing cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
Fast-growing cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than slower-growing cell lines.
Fast-growing cell lines with `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than slower-growing cell lines.

analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.282 vs -0.245; effect -0.037 (p=2.22e-24).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: High-growth lines had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.361 vs -0.324; effect -0.037 (p=1.50e-24).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In systematic subgroup screening, `braf_v600e == 1` plus `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964` marks a BRAF-dependent subgroup with very negative `dependency_BRAF`.
In systematic subgroup screening, `egfr_amplification == 1`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, and `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` mark an EGFR-dependent subgroup.
In systematic subgroup screening, `erbb2_amplification == 1`, `lineage == breast`, and `ifn_gamma_signature >= 0.519` mark an ERBB2-dependent subgroup.
In systematic subgroup screening, `kras_mutation == 1`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, and `stk11_loss == 1` mark a KRAS-dependent subgroup.
In systematic subgroup screening, `nf1_loss == 1`, `aneuploidy_score <= 0.256`, and `mutation_burden <= 3.230` mark a RIT1-dependent subgroup.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Best BRAF subgroup check had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.829 vs -0.296; effect -0.532 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Best EGFR exploratory subgroup had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.669 vs -0.277; effect -0.391 (p=1.27e-71).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Best ERBB2 exploratory subgroup had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.760 vs -0.293; effect -0.467 (p=9.53e-108).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Best KRAS exploratory subgroup had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.601 vs -0.323; effect -0.278 (p=3.71e-34).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Best RIT1 exploratory subgroup had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.407 vs -0.265; effect -0.142 (p=8.04e-12).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In systematic subgroup screening, the complete `lineage == colorectal`, `apc_mutation == 1`, `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` subgroup is the strongest KIF18A-dependent subgroup.
In exploratory screening, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, `lineage == ovarian`, and `rnaseq_SLFN11_log2_tpm >= 4.439` mark a TMED10-dependent subgroup.
In exploratory screening, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm >= 5.669`, `media_serum_pct <= 5.000`, and `lineage_subtype == squamous` mark a DHX9-dependent subgroup.
In exploratory screening, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, `lineage == lung`, and `rnaseq_SLFN11_log2_tpm >= 4.439` mark a POLR2A-dependent subgroup.
In exploratory screening, `myc_amplification == 1`, `growth_rate_doublings_per_day >= 0.964`, and `lineage == pancreatic` mark an RPL11-dependent subgroup.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic KIF18A search selected the colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT subgroup: mean -1.112 vs -0.253; effect -0.859 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Best TMED10 exploratory subgroup had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.345 vs -0.254; effect -0.091 (p=3.34e-05).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.
analyses.result_summary: Best DHX9 exploratory subgroup had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.427 vs -0.333; effect -0.094 (p=2.70e-05).

analyses.code: Welch two-sample contrast of the named subgroup versus its complement; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `lineage`, `braf_v600e == 1` remains associated with more negative `dependency_BRAF`.
After adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `lineage`, `egfr_amplification == 1` remains associated with more negative `dependency_EGFR`.
After adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `lineage`, `erbb2_amplification == 1` remains associated with more negative `dependency_ERBB2`.
After adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `lineage`, `kras_mutation == 1` remains associated with more negative `dependency_KRAS`.
After adjusting for `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`, `cas9_activity_score`, and `lineage`, `nf1_loss == 1` remains associated with more negative `dependency_RIT1`.

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `braf_v600e` on `dependency_BRAF` was -0.555 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `egfr_amplification` on `dependency_EGFR` was -0.349 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `erbb2_amplification` on `dependency_ERBB2` was -0.432 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `kras_mutation` on `dependency_KRAS` was -0.309 (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `nf1_loss` on `dependency_RIT1` was -0.095 (p=5.12e-69).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The KIF18A effect is a three-way interaction: `lineage == colorectal`, `apc_mutation == 1`, and `wnt_activity_score >= 0.819` remain associated with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` after lower-order terms, growth, and Cas9 activity are modeled.

The RPL11 MYC-context effect is not just growth: `myc_amplification == 1` combined with `copy_number_8q24 >= 0.428` remains associated with more negative `dependency_RPL11` after growth and Cas9 adjustment.
Increasing `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` as a continuous effect.
Increasing `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10` as a continuous effect.
Increasing `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` as a continuous effect.

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted three-way OLS coefficient for colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT on `dependency_KIF18A` was -0.817 (p=8.77e-228).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted MYC-amplification by high-8q24 interaction coefficient on `dependency_RPL11` was -0.037 (p=1.06e-01).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Continuous growth-rate slope for `dependency_POLR2A` was -0.305 per doubling/day (p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Continuous growth-rate slope for `dependency_TMED10` was -0.101 per doubling/day (p=1.11e-38).

analyses.code: Ordinary least squares with the stated predictors; p-values use a large-sample normal approximation for the coefficient z-score.
analyses.result_summary: Continuous growth-rate slope for `dependency_DHX9` was -0.109 per doubling/day (p=2.73e-44).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final synthesis: the strongest dependency-outcome-specific discoveries are canonical oncogene addiction for BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, KRAS, an NF1/RIT1 relationship, a MYC/8q24/RPL11 relationship, and a noncanonical colorectal APC-mutant high-WNT KIF18A dependency.
Final synthesis: TMED10, DHX9, and POLR2A show stronger evidence for growth or screen-context dependency than for a single driver-genotype dependency subgroup.

analyses.code: Synthesis of the strongest pre-specified and systematically searched contrasts.
analyses.result_summary: The largest dependency-specific subgroup effect was for colorectal/APC-mutant/high-WNT `dependency_KIF18A` with effect -0.859, alongside canonical driver effects BRAF -0.555, EGFR -0.349, ERBB2 -0.432, KRAS -0.307, RIT1/NF1 -0.096, and RPL11/MYC-8q24 -0.112.

analyses.code: Synthesis of continuous growth-rate models and exploratory subgroup screens.
analyses.result_summary: Growth-rate slopes were negative for POLR2A (-0.305), TMED10 (-0.101), and DHX9 (-0.109); exploratory subgroup hits for TMED10 and DHX9 were much smaller than the KIF18A and canonical driver effects.
